steps.title: Identify the hemisphere volume formula
steps.content: The volume of a sphere is $\frac{4}{3}\pi r^3$. Since a hemisphere is half of a sphere, we get: $$V = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3 = \frac{2}{3}\pi r^3$$

steps.title: Substitute radius 70 and evaluate
steps.content: Substitute $r = 70$ inches into the formula: $$V = \frac{2}{3}\pi(70)^3$$ Calculate $70^3 = 70 \times 70 \times 70 = 4,900 \times 70 = 343,000$. Then: $$V = \frac{2}{3}\pi(343,000) = \frac{686,000}{3}\pi$$ Simplifying the fraction: $\frac{686,000}{3} \approx 228,667$, so: $$V \approx 228,667\pi$$

steps.title: Multiply by π and match to the answer choice
steps.content: Use the approximation $\pi \approx 3.14$: $$V \approx 228,667 \times 3.14 \approx 718,000$$ The answer choice closest to 718,000 is 718,400. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
